reasoning:
The 360-day average threshold is fixed based on data preceding 2026-02-01. This window includes January 2026, which saw unprecedented massacres on January 8 and 9 (with thousands reported killed). Because the threshold is computed by taking the 360-day sum and dividing by 12, a huge spike in January will drastically inflate the 30-day average threshold (from ~76 to possibly over 300+). 

Resolution 1 (Feb 8, 2026) evaluates the 30 days prior (Jan 9 to Feb 7). This window includes January 9, meaning it captures a significant portion of the massive fatalities. mathematically, a window containing half or more of an extreme outlier will easily exceed the 1/12th diluted impact of that outlier on the average. Thus, Res 1 is highly likely to be > average (YES).

Resolution 2 (Mar 3, 2026) evaluates Feb 1 to Mar 2. This window completely misses the January massacres. Therefore, it will be evaluated against a heavily inflated threshold using only the "tail end" of the crackdown. Unless February sustains thousands of deaths, it will fall short of the new elevated average. Thus, Res 2 is unlikely to be > average (NO).
